note: Qualified Taxila-valley adapter: the installed Attock field is an explicit distant market proxy rather than the excavated city site.
Qualified AD 1 port-name adapter: Oxford identifies Mouza as probably modern Mocha while noting that the ancient site remains unexcavated.
Qualified Gulf-of-Khambhat adapter: Oxford identifies Barygaza with Bharuch near the Narmada mouth rather than the direct installed field.
Contested Kodungallur-region port-name adapter: earlier scholarship places Muziris at Kodungallur but current archaeology also considers nearby Pattanam.
Qualified direct Taman-peninsula city adapter: Phanagoria is the ancient city on Taman while Panticapaeum was on the Kerch side of the strait.
Qualified regional-node adapter: the installed Angkor key is the project Mekong-basin proxy and must not assert later Angkor at AD 1.
Qualified Oc Eo-region growth adapter: early occupation and canal context are attested but the exact Oc Eo footprint and AD 1 urban scale remain contested.
Qualified Basin-of-Mexico adapter: the installed Azcapotzalco field is the project Cuicuilco proxy rather than a claim about the later Aztec-period name.
Qualified north-coast adapter: the installed Chan Chan field represents the contested Gallinazo Virú-Valley context and does not claim the later Chan Chan city existed at AD 1.
Qualified Red-Sea port adapter: the installed Massawa field is the project Adulis harbor proxy rather than an assertion that the later city name applied at AD 1.
Qualified Han commandery adapter: the installed Thang Long field is a Red-River Jiaozhi proxy and must not project the 1010 CE capital name into AD 1.
Qualified Zapotec highland adapter: the installed Zaachila field is the project Monte Albán proxy rather than a claim about the later settlement name.
Qualified coastal-region adapter: the installed Martaban field is the project Mon precursor market proxy and must not project the later port name into AD 1.
Qualified archipelago-region adapter: the installed Maynila field is the project Philippine SoP anchor and must not project the later Manila name into AD 1.
Qualified Maya-lowland adapter: the installed Noh Petén field is the project Tikal-zone market proxy rather than an assertion about the later Itza capital name.
Qualified coastal-region adapter: the installed Surabaya field is the project early Javanese market anchor and must not project the later city name into AD 1.
Qualified Iranian-region adapter: the installed Tabriz field is the project Atropatenian market anchor and must not project the later city name into AD 1.
Qualified straits-region adapter: the installed Temasek field is the project early Malay market anchor and must not project the later settlement name into AD 1.
